findings.title: Dead 'gauges' key in context_builder.gd build_full_context()
findings.detail: run.get('gauges', {}) always returns {} — the 4-gauge system is SUPPRESSED per bible v2.4. This dead key propagates through the full context and is iterated as no-ops by fallback_pool.gd, difficulty_adapter.gd, tone_controller.gd. Also get_critical_gauges() is dead production code (only called from tests).
findings.impact: LLM context pollution, dead code maintenance burden, processor loops on empty dict.

findings.title: Queue tasks P0-DEADCODE-1 through P0-DEADCODE-5, P1-CONSTANTS-1/2/3 already implemented
findings.detail: Verified in code: ELEMENTS/ESSENCE_*/FLUX_* removed from merlin_constants.gd. TRIADE_ prefix removed from merlin_store.gd. Dead state keys (bestiole, ogham_fragments) not found. Bestiole .gd files deleted. OGHAM_FULL_SPECS, BIOMES, ACTION_VERBS, MOS_CONVERGENCE, TRUST_TIERS already present. Queue is stale.
findings.impact: Bot wastes cycle time trying to implement already-done tasks. Queue misleads priority.

findings.title: OllamaBackend.check_model_available() creates a redundant HTTP connection
findings.detail: check_available() and check_model_available() both establish independent HTTP connections to Ollama. check_model_available() re-reads /api/tags body even if check_available() already confirmed the server is up. They are called in sequence in merlin_ai.gd._try_init_ollama().
findings.impact: Unnecessary double connect on startup. Minor latency.

findings.title: stats.last_ttft_ms never updated in non-streaming OllamaBackend path
findings.detail: stats dict declares 'last_ttft_ms': 0 but _blocking_generate() never sets it. Only the streaming path (_blocking_stream) could measure TTFT, but it also doesn't update last_ttft_ms. Telemetry reads always return 0.

findings.title: Dead gauges context in decision_history_registry.gd and merlin_omniscient.gd
findings.detail: Decision history records 'gauges_before' and 'gauges_after' for every card played — both always empty dicts. This bloats the cross-run memory JSON. merlin_omniscient.gd also passes gauges_before/after.
findings.impact: Persistent storage bloat. Cross-run memory JSON grows with empty gauge fields.

findings.title: GroqBackend: no graceful degradation when GROQ_API_KEY is missing
findings.detail: If api_key is empty after checking ProjectSettings + OS env, generate_async() will attempt HTTP calls with no Authorization header, resulting in 401 errors logged as 'Groq HTTP 401'. No explicit 'key missing' warning before the request attempt.
findings.impact: Confusing error messages. Minor UX issue in dev.

findings.title: mg_de_du_destin.gd is referenced — P0-DEADCODE-5 should NOT delete it
findings.detail: TransitionBiome.gd:526 and minigame_registry.gd:115,147 actively preload and use mg_de_du_destin.gd as a 'chance' minigame (D6, not D20). The bible suppressed the D20 resolution system, not this minigame. Task P0-DEADCODE-5 was wrong to include it.
findings.impact: If deleted, minigame registry crashes. Clarified: file should be KEPT.

findings.title: Brain profile fallback chain is robust: Ollama -> Groq -> BitNet -> MerlinLLM
findings.detail: The _init_local_models() cascade correctly tries all backends in order with graceful fallback. Warmup retry after 30s is implemented. Cold-start timeout (90s) vs warm timeout (25s) distinction is present. Brain swarm profiles (NANO/SINGLE/SINGLE+/DUAL/TRIPLE/QUAD) auto-detect from available RAM.
findings.impact: Positive finding: LLM pipeline is resilient.

summary: LLM pipeline is fundamentally sound with good fallback chains and JSON repair. Main issues are dead gauge code left over from the suppressed 4-gauge system, and a stale feature queue where P0-P1 tasks are already implemented. Priority fix: clean dead gauge context from AI layer + archive stale queue tasks.
task_implemented: LLM-GAUGE-CLEANUP — Remove dead gauges context from context_builder.gd + mark stale queue tasks completed
